October 8th, 1988.

The Fall Pep Rally had wrapped hours ago. It was well past 11 PM, and the campus was practically deserted. From the empty hallways of the athletic wing, Patricia Steele wandered toward the cheerleaders' locker room.

Her steps were steady enough, but her head felt just a little floaty — a side effect of all those "mixed drinks" the squad had been passing around backstage. Everyone else had already bolted to the after-party across town. But Patti? She wasn't really feeling it. Her head was pounding lightly, her curls still damp with sweat, and the idea of squeezing into some crowded apartment felt exhausting.

The metal door creaked open. The locker room was quiet — dim overhead lights buzzing faintly, the scent of hairspray and perfume lingering. Patti stepped inside, her cheer uniform still clinging to her skin: that cropped white top, the red pleated skirt riding high on her thighs, white knee socks slouched down at her calves.

She tossed her duffel bag onto the bench… but then paused. A strange warmth prickled beneath her skin. Her thighs rubbed together instinctively as her fingers toyed with the hem of her skirt. The leftover buzz from the drinks, the quiet hum of the empty building, the faint echo of A-ha still looping in her head—it all stirred something… needy.

She glanced around, blue eyes scanning the empty room. No one. Just lockers, tiled floors, and the soft creak of the old air vents. A sly smirk curled on her lips as her hand drifted down, sliding beneath the edge of her skirt. Her fingers slipped past the waistband of her underwear—teasing, exploring her pussy, as her breath caught softly in her throat.
